name: Segmentation/Tracking · Failure-Taxonomy Variant
full_intro_template: P1 Problem setup: In <segmentation/tracking task>, reliable predictions are required for <application scenario> under <noise/occlusion/domain shift>.
P2 Gap statement: Existing methods are strong on average metrics but fragile on <hard subset> where <failure trigger> appears.
P3 Why prior work fails: Most pipelines optimize global score but ignore boundary or temporal consistency constraints.
P4 Reframing: We decouple representation learning and structure refinement with explicit supervision on <critical signal>.
P5 Method preview: Stage-1 captures coarse semantics; stage-2 enforces geometric/temporal consistency.
P6 Evidence contract: We report matched-budget gains on <metric1/metric2> with <mean ± std> and significance test.
P7 Cost disclosure: We include latency/memory/compute overhead against strongest baseline.
P8 Limitations: We discuss failure cases in <rare categories/extreme motion/small objects>.
P9 Contributions: (i) reliable formulation, (ii) practical mechanism, (iii) detailed diagnostics and ablations.
abstract_template: A1: Define task and deployment setting with one measurable constraint.
A2: State precise gap and why prior work fails in that regime.
A3: Present method/protocol and strongest baseline delta under matched budget.
A4: Add uncertainty (mean±std/significance) and one limitation boundary.
method_template: M1 Define input-output and notation for task entities and labels.
M2 Explain stage-1 backbone and representation objective.
M3 Explain stage-2 refinement and consistency constraints.
M4 Describe training objective terms and weighting strategy.
M5 Report complexity, inference latency, and memory footprint.
M6 Provide reproducibility details: seeds, hyperparameters, and implementation settings.
experiments_template: E1 Datasets and protocol: include split policy and leakage checks.
E2 Main comparison with strongest baselines under matched budget.
E3 Statistical reliability: mean ± std, confidence intervals, significance tests.
E4 Ablation on each module and error attribution.
E5 Hard-subset and failure-case analysis with representative examples.
E6 Efficiency trade-off: quality vs latency/memory/compute.
falsifiable_claims: If <core module> is removed, performance on <hard subset> should decrease by >= <delta>.
Under matched training/inference budget, <our method> should outperform <strong baseline> on <metric>.
delta_vs_strongest_baseline: Compared with <strong baseline>, we add <mechanism> and obtain <delta> under matched budget and protocol.
limitations_or_failure_cases: We explicitly analyze failure cases under <scenario> and report why the method is not dominant there.
contribution_schema: Contribution: <type> | Evidence: <verifiable artifact> | Risk/Boundary: <where it may fail>

operational_definition.B: resource or reliability metric reported jointly

paragraph_cards.template: P1 Task value and realistic disturbances.
paragraph_cards.evidence_hook: Link this paragraph to a concrete artifact: Table/Figure/Theorem/Ablation.

anti_pattern_examples.bad: We achieve SOTA on all benchmarks.
anti_pattern_examples.better: Under matched training/inference budgets, we outperform strong baselines on <metric> over <subset>.

anti_pattern_examples.bad: This is the first work to study <task>.
anti_pattern_examples.better: To the best of our knowledge, this is one of the first unified treatments under <assumptions>.

anti_pattern_examples.bad: Our method is reliable.
anti_pattern_examples.better: We quantify stability on <stress test> and report failure cases on <hard subset>.

statistical_reliability_requirements: Report mean±std over >=3 random seeds when stochastic training is used.
State whether significance testing was performed and which test was used.
cost_disclosure_requirements: Report at least one cost metric from: fps, latency, memory.
Compare cost under matched hardware/batch settings.

reorder_profiles.description: context -> gap -> idea -> evidence -> limitation -> contributions
context -> evidence -> gap -> idea -> limitation -> contributions
context -> protocol -> gap -> idea -> evidence -> contributions

conclusion_template: C1 Summarize core mechanism and why it improves stability.
C2 Reiterate quantitative gains with cost boundaries.
C3 Clarify limitations and near-term extensions.
